case_name: Stephanie Clark v. SSM Healthcare St. Louis, d/b/a SSM Health St. Joseph Hospital, Wentzville, Dawn Holemon, M.D., Lee M. Freund, D.O., and St. Charles Emergency Group, LLC.

summary.brief: Stephanie Clark v. SSM Healthcare St. Louis, d/b/a SSM Health St. Joseph Hospital, Wentzville, Dawn Holemon, M.D., Lee M. Freund, D.O., and St. Charles Emergency Group, LLC.. Medical malpractice case involving surgery orthopaedic.

evidence_index.text: D.G. had become suicidal and was taken to the SSM Health St. Joseph Hospital emergency room in Wentzville, Missouri where she came under the care of Respondents.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Psychiatric evaluation took place and D.G. was released later that evening.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: D.G. hung herself from a pull-up bar the next morning and died a few days later.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We reverse on all three bases. First, as to causation, by imposing on Clark the burden to show that the Respondents first inflicted an injury upon D.G.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Dr. Caine testified that D.G.’s death was foreseeable and that the negligent actions and omissions of Dr. Holemon, Dr. Freund, SSM Healthcare St. Louis, and St. Charles Emergency Group caused or contributed to cause her death.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Eric Caine

evidence_index.text: Dr. Betz testified that Dr. Freund’s actions fell below the standard of care because he did not provide a safe discharge plan.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Marian Betz

evidence_index.text: Summary judgment is appropriate when the movant establishes there are no genuine issues of material fact and are entitled to judgment as a matter of law.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: We therefore reverse because the trial court erred in relying on the 2020 version of the aggravating circumstances statute and Clark alleged sufficient facts to preclude summary judgment as to her aggravating circumstances damages claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: For the reasons set forth above, we reverse the summary judgments entered in this case.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: D.G. was suspended from school for fighting, became physically aggressive with her mother, and threatened suicide. Police were called, and she was taken to the hospital.

reveal.result_summary: D.G. was evaluated and found to have suicidal ideation but was discharged
reveal.clinical_significance: Discharge decision made despite suicidal ideation

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with suicidal ideation

key_events.event: Suicide risk assessment by intake clinician

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with suicidal ideation
decision_points.prompt: What should be the discharge plan for a patient with acute suicidal ideation?

options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care for acute suicidal ideation to ensure patient safety
Inadequate given the patient's acute risk

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant discharged D.G. with outpatient follow-up
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Based on brief assessment by intake clinician

expected_action_court.description: D.G. should have been admitted for psychiatric observation
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated admission was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Discharge was inappropriate given the patient's suicidal ideation and risk
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been admitted for observation
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: D.G. committed suicide the following morning

key_findings: Trial court initially found no causation
Appellate court reversed summary judgment, finding causation was a disputed issue

allegations.allegation: Failure to provide safe discharge plan
